title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: VLCC Tonne Days from the AG to China
VLCC Tonne days growth from AG to China has decreased to the lowest level since the end of 2022
During the first week of October, the VLCC MEG China rates experienced a downward revision, amid hopes of a recovery. Looking at the demand, growth rates are still significantly lower than what they were the previous year leading to a negative sentiment in the crude freight market. Moreover, there are concerns about oil demand as oil prices dropped early in October due to Saudi Arabian oil supply cuts and a drop in US crude oil inventories.

children.title: Chart of the Week: VLCC Tonne Days from the AG to China
section_path: Chart of the Week: VLCC Tonne Days from the AG to China
children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week: VLCC Tonne Days from the AG to China
children.summary:
VLCC Tonne days growth from AG to China has decreased to the lowest level since the end of 2022
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-06T142709.826.png | assets/2023-10-06_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-06t142709826_7baa02bd8069.png
During the first week of October, the VLCC MEG China rates experienced a downward revision, amid hopes of a recovery. Looking at the demand, growth rates are still significantly lower than what they were the previous year leading to a negative sentiment in the crude freight market.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
Market Rates (WS)
‘Dirty' WS VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax Mixed
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-06T142928.176.png | assets/2023-10-06_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-06t142928176_a3373711538b.png
The VLCC MEG-China rates dropped again in early October, while the Suezmax and Aframax segments remained stable. VLCC MEG-China freight rates decreased to WS 40, down 20% from the previous week, although they remain 8% higher than a month ago.

children.title: ‘Product' WS LR2 Steady
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-06T143034.364.png | assets/2023-10-06_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-06t143034364_a669b26214ea.png
LR2 AG freight rates remained stable above WS130, with a 4% increase over a month. Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-06T143116.358.png | assets/2023-10-06_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-06t143116358_060db119292b.png
Panamax Carib-to-USG rates fell to 134 WS, down 60% from a year ago, with the downward trend continuing since the end of Week 27.

children.title: ‘ Clean ' MR Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-06T143224.404.png | assets/2023-10-06_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-06t143224404_8c06d3358f57.png
MR1 rates for the Baltic continent increased to WS 250, up 20% from the previous week. MR2 rates for shipments from the continent to the U.S.

children.title: 'Dirty' (#vessels) - Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-06T143412.190.png | assets/2023-10-06_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-06t143412190_be85dac738bf.png
In early October, crude oil tanker supply showed a mixed picture. The supply trend for the Ras Tanura VLCC increased, while other crude tanker categories saw a downward correction from the end of the previous month.

children.title: MR1 (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-06T143622.653.png | assets/2023-10-06_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-06t143622653_a7627be58043.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The number of recorded ships shows signs of increasing again, although there was a drop at the end of September. The recent figure brings the trend closer to the annual average.

children.title: ‘Dirty' Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-06T143749.876.png | assets/2023-10-06_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-06t143749876_d46474000654.png
Dirty tonne days: The demand for Aframax increased, while VLCC and Suezmax faced a persistent downward correction, posing a challenge for the freight market sentiment in October.

children.title: ‘Clean' Panamax / MR Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-06T143834.756.png | assets/2023-10-06_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-06t143834756_b3c7fb906d26.png
Panamax tonne days: The growth rate sharply declined in early October and continues to trend downwards. Clean MR tonne days: The MR1 segment experienced a record low growth rate throughout the year, while demand sentiment for MR2 softened.
